entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Photoperiod insensitive sorghum cultivar (Sorghum bicolor L. Moench)

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental temperature affecting crop growth and yield

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Environmental rainfall affecting crop growth and yield

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: Country in the Sudanian zone where the study was conducted

entities.name: APSIM
entities.description: Agricultural Production Systems Simulator used for crop simulation and evaluation

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: TOTAL BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of biological material produced by the crop

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of crop yield to the amount of water used

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: Nutrient application used to influence crop growth and yield

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: Phenological stage of the sorghum crop

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL MATURITY
entities.description: Phenological stage of the sorghum crop

entities.name: SOWING DATE
entities.description: The date on which seeds are planted, used for model calibration

entities.name: MEAN BIAS ERROR
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the performance of the APSIM model

entities.name: ROOT MEAN SQUARE ERROR
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the performance of the APSIM model

relationships.description: Increased temperature leads to a decline in grain yield
Decreased rainfall leads to a significant decline in grain yield
Used to simulate the growth and response of photoperiod insensitive sorghum
Used to evaluate and predict grain yield patterns
Used to evaluate and predict total biomass
Application rates affect the grain yield of sorghum
Cultivar studied in the Sudanian zone of Mali
Used to measure the accuracy of APSIM model predictions for flowering and maturity
Used to measure the accuracy of APSIM model predictions for biomass and yield
Used as a variable for model calibration of the sorghum cultivar
